name: From Villamanrique de la Condesa to the Guadiamar River — circular through El Aljarafe
name_original: De Villamanrique de la Condesa al Río Guadiamar — circular por El Aljarafe
region_intro: Hiking around Villamanrique De La Condesa offers access to diverse landscapes at the edge of Doñana National Park. The region features extensive marshes, pine forests, and cultivated fields, providing varied terrain for outdoor activities. The Guadiamar Green Corridor, following the Guadiamar River, is a prominent natural feature, characterized by its protected riverbanks and diverse ecosystems. The area's low elevation gain makes many routes accessible for various fitness levels. Best hiking trails around Villamanrique De La Condesa The most popular hiking route is Circular route around the banks of the Guadiamar River - through the Protected Landscape Green Corridor of the Guadiamar, a 10.6 miles (17.0 km) trail that takes 4 hours 17 minutes to complete. This moderate route explores the protected Guadiamar Green Corridor. Another top favourite among local hikers is Aznalcazar and Ribera Rio Guadiamar — circular through the village and surroundings, a moderate 9.1 miles (14.6 km) path. This trail offers views of the village and the Guadiamar River. Local hikers also love the From Villamanrique de la Condesa to the Guadiamar River — circular through El Aljarafe, a 5.7 miles (9.2 km) trail leading through the El Aljarafe region, often completed in about 2 hours 22 minutes. Hiking around Villamanrique De La Condesa is defined by its proximity to Doñana National Park, the Guadiamar Green Corridor, and its varied terrain of marshes and pine forests. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy walks to moderate excursions. The routes in Villamanrique De La Condesa are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 20 reviews. More than 100 hikers have used komoot to explore Villamanrique De La Condesa's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available around Villamanrique De La Condesa?
A: There are over 20 hiking trails around Villamanrique De La Condesa, offering a variety of experiences. The majority of these routes are considered moderate, with some easier options available for those seeking a more relaxed walk.

Q: What kind of landscapes can I expect to see while hiking in this region?
A: Hiking around Villamanrique De La Condesa offers diverse landscapes, from the extensive marshes and lush pine forests characteristic of the Doñana National Park gateway to cultivated fields. The region is also defined by the protected Guadiamar Green Corridor, which follows the banks of the Guadiamar River.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes available?
A: Yes, many routes in the area are circular. For instance, you can explore the protected Guadiamar Green Corridor on the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1006557384/circular-route-around-the-banks-of-the-guadiamar-river-through-the-protected-landscape-green-corridor-of-the-guadiamar" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Circular route around the banks of the Guadiamar River&lt;/a&gt;, which is a moderate 10.6-mile trail.

Q: What natural attractions can I discover along the trails?
A: The region is rich in natural beauty. You can explore the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3987366" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Guadiamar Green Corridor Protected Landscape&lt;/a&gt;, observe wildlife from a &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5379343" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hut with views of the lagoon&lt;/a&gt;, or wander through the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5301305" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pine Forest of Doñana Natural Park&lt;/a&gt;. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5379374" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Dehesa de Covarrubias&lt;/a&gt; is another notable forest area.

Q: Are there any historical or cultural landmarks to see while hiking?
A: Yes, the area offers cultural points of interest. The historic Vado del Quema, a traditional crossing point for pilgrims to El Rocío, is a significant site. You can also find the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1824240" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vado del Quema Shrine&lt;/a&gt; nearby. The Ethnographic Interpretation Centre Camino del Rocío in town provides further context on this pilgrimage.

Q: Are there family-friendly hiking options in Villamanrique De La Condesa?
A: Many of the paths around Villamanrique De La Condesa are suitable for various fitness levels, including families. The low elevation gain across most routes makes them accessible for a pleasant outing with children. Look for trails classified as 'easy' for the most family-friendly experience.

Q: What do other hikers say about the trails in Villamanrique De La Condesa?
A: The trails in Villamanrique De La Condesa are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 20 reviews. Hikers often praise the varied terrain, the natural beauty of the Guadiamar Green Corridor, and the peaceful atmosphere at the edge of Doñana National Park.

Q: Is there parking available near the trailheads?
A: While specific parking areas for every trailhead are not detailed, Villamanrique de la Condesa is a town that caters to visitors exploring the surrounding natural areas. Generally, you can find parking within the town or at designated visitor centers like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5379366" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Dehesa de Abajo Visitor Center&lt;/a&gt;, which serves as a good starting point for many excursions.

Q: Can I bring my dog on the hiking trails?
A: The natural areas around Villamanrique De La Condesa, including parts of the Guadiamar Green Corridor and Dehesa Boyal, are generally welcoming to dogs. However, when hiking near or within protected areas like Doñana National Park, it's always best to keep dogs on a leash and check for any specific local regulations to protect wildlife.

Q: What is a good moderate hike that explores the local surroundings?
A: A great moderate option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1006579955/aznalcazar-and-ribera-rio-guadiamar-circular-through-the-village-and-surroundings" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Aznalcazar and Ribera Rio Guadiamar — circular through the village and surroundings&lt;/a&gt;. This 9.1-mile path offers views of the village and the Guadiamar River, providing a good sense of the local landscape.

Q: Are there any visitor centers or facilities nearby to help plan my hike?
A: Yes, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5379366" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Dehesa de Abajo Visitor Center (Puebla del Río)&lt;/a&gt; is an excellent resource for learning about the region's unique characteristics and ecological evolution, especially concerning Doñana National Park. It can serve as a starting point for your outdoor adventures.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Villamanrique De La Condesa?
A: The best time for hiking in Villamanrique De La Condesa is typically during the cooler months, from autumn through spring (October to May). Summers can be very hot in Andalusia, making longer hikes less comfortable. Spring offers pleasant temperatures and vibrant natural scenery.
tips: When you cross the Río de Guadiamar you will find an explanatory sign indicating that you are in a place of pilgrimage. The river, where it carries water, offers a green and beautiful space!
Famous pass through which, once a year, pilgrims pass on their way to El Rocío. If we find too much water, we can always use the bridge a few meters upstream.
